results.rendered_html: brahma—yajña m. ‘Vedic offering’, recitation of portions of the Veda and sacred books at the Saṃdhyā,  ŚBr. ;  ĀśvGṛ.  &c. (one of the 5 Mahā-yajñas or great devotional acts,  Mn. iii, 69 ; 70 ; cf.  IW. 194 ;  RTL. 393 )

results.rendered_html:
brahmayajña (1. brahman + yajña) m. Andachtsopfer d. i. Hersagung eines heiligen Textes, heiliges Studium  AK. 2,7,14 .  H. 821 .  ŚAT. BR. 11,5,6,1 . 3 . yatsvādhyāyamadhīte sa ˚yajñaḥ  ĀŚV. GṚHY. 3,1,3 . brahmayajño vā eṣa yatpūrveṣāṃ cayanam  MAITRYUP. 1,1 .  MÜLLER, SL. 356 . 458 . Schol. zu  AV. PRĀT. 4,107 . adhyāpanaṃ ˚yajñaḥ  M. 3,70  ( Verz. d. Oxf. H. 267,b,42 ).  HARIV. 11695  (vgl. 11806 ).  Verz. d. Oxf. H. 12,b,19 . 26 . 85,a,39 . 265,a,4 . 276,b,23 . Neben japa  MAHĪDH. _zu_ VS. 32,3 . ˚saṃhitā Schol. zu  VS. PRĀT. 4,175 . brahmayajñādividhi  Verz. d. B. H. No. 135 .
— Vgl. brahmasattra.
